memoryArtifacts.headline: Robert Rose 1594-1665 Memorial Plaque
memoryArtifacts.description: Robert was one of the group considered to be Puritans. Robert and Margery settled in an Indian settlement known as Pyquag, which was soon renamed Watertown. The name was changed to Wethersfield in 1635. Robert is seen in many original land records and histories of the town. He was constable in 1640 and Representitive in 1641-1643 in Wethersfield. He was a Freeman in Stratford in 1648. Some time between 1644 and 1646, Robert and some of his children moved to Branford, Connecticut. It is believed that he had a tiff with the minister of the Congregational Church in Wethersfield, and that is one of the reasons he and several of his neighbors decided to move. He is also seen in many deeds and town records in Branford, Connecticut.

zh.lifeSummary: Robert Rose Sr於1594年7月1日出生在英國素福爾可艾爾姆斯維爾。他於大約1619年與Margery Everard在英國結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1665年4月4日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文布藍福德享年70歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文諾斯 布藍福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Robert Rose Sr于1594年7月1日出生在英国素福爾可艾爾姆斯維爾。他于大约1619年与Margery Everard在英国结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1665年4月4日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文布藍福德享年70岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文諾斯 布藍福德。

nameMeanings.name: Rose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Danish, and German: from the name of the flower, Middle English, Old French, Middle High German rose (from Latin rosa), in various applications. In part, it is a topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house bearing the sign of the rose. It is also found, especially in Europe, as a nickname for a man with a ‘rosy’ complexion (compare 4 below). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Rózsa (see Rozsa ), Slovak Róža and Czech Roza . Compare 6 below and French Larose 2.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Rohese, Roese, later Rose, Royse (ancient Germanic Hrodohaidis, Rothaid, composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + haid(is) ‘kind, sort’).
English and Scottish: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rosenbach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans.
German: habitational name from any of numerous places so named (see   Rose   and   Bach  ).

variants.name: Roser
meanings: German: topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew (see Rose 1), with the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant, or an occupational name for a rose grower, or a nickname for a lover of roses.
German (Röser): habitational name for someone from any of the places called Rös, Roes, or Rösa in Bavaria, Rhineland, and Saxony.
German (Röser): variant of Rösser (see Rosser 3).

variants.name: Roseman
meanings: English (Middlesex): from the Middle English female personal name Rosamunde (ancient Germanic Rosmund, from hros ‘horse’ + mund ‘protection’). The personal name was widely understood in the Middle Ages as Latin rosa munda ‘pure rose’ and therefore as an epithet of the Virgin Mary.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name or name adopted by the husband of a woman bearing the Yiddish personal name Royze (see Rose 9).
Americanized form of German Rosemann or Rosmann (see Rossmann ).

variants.name: Roos
meanings: Dutch (also De Roos): topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a rose, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who grew roses, from roos ‘rose’.
Dutch: from the female personal name Rosa (from Latin rosa ‘rose’).
Dutch: nickname from roos ‘erysipelas’, an infection which causes reddening of the skin and scalp, applied presumably to someone with a ruddy complexion.

variants.name: Reisen
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): possibly an artificial name from northeastern Yiddish reyzn ‘roses’ (standard Yiddish royzn); see Rose 9.
German: unexplained.

variants.name: Rosado
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Carlos, Rafael, Angel, Manuel, Francisco, Ramon, Miguel, Jorge, Pedro.
Spanish: nickname for someone with a notably pink and white complexion, from Spanish rosado ‘pink’, Late Latin rosatus, a derivative of rosa (see Rose ).

variants.name: Roesel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Otto.
German (Rösel): diminutive of Rose .

variants.name: Rosette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Arturo, Cirilo, Consuelo, Cristina, Eloy, Eufrosina, Felipe, Jesus, Jorge, Manuel.

French: from a pet form of the female personal name   Rose   6 ‘rose’, or a habitational name from any of several places called (La) Rosette, e.g. in Yonne, denoting a place where roses grow. This surname is also found in the West Indies and Mauritius.
Americanized form of Norwegian   Roseth  .

variants.name: Rozsa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Sandor, Tibor, Zoltan, Zsuzsanna, Balint, Gabor, Gyula, Imre, Istvan, Katalin, Laszlo, Miklos.  German Armin, Erwin, Theodor.

Hungarian (Rózsa): from the female personal name Rózsa, from the vocabulary word rózsa ‘rose’. In some cases the surname might derive from an old male personal name of the same form. Compare Roja 2 and Rose 1.

variants.name: Roese
meanings: German (Röse):
variant of   Rose  .
possibly also a habitational name from a place called Rösa near Bitterfeld.

nameMeanings.name: Sr
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Abha, Amit, Gopa, Partha, Prabhat, Sandip, Sanjiv, Shakti, Shyama, Sonali, Sumana, Sumit.  Korean Chae, Jin, Jong, Kheng, Ki Suk, Soon, Sun Kyu, Wan.
German: variant of   Sauer  .
Indian (West Bengal) and Bangladeshi: nickname from Sanskrit šūra ‘brave’.

variants.name: Sauer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Heinz, Otto, Achim, Dieter, Erwin, Hans, Helmut, Reinhold, Erna, Florian, Fritz.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for an embittered or cantankerous person, from Middle High German sūr, German sauer ‘sour’. In Jewish families, it is mainly artificial, assigned at random by Austrian clerks. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), the Netherlands, Denmark, Poland, Czechia, Croatia, and Slovenia.
Czech and Slovenian (Šauer): from German Schauer . In Slovenia, it is in part of Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) origin.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."
On April 24, 1638, a company of five-hundred English Puritans led by the Reverend John Davenport and Theophilus Eaton, a wealthy London merchant, sailed into the harbor.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Robert Rose Sr was born on 1 July 1594, in Elmswell, Suffolk, England. He married Margery Everard about 1619, in England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 4 April 1665, in Branford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 70, and was buried in North Branford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Robert Rose Sr est né le 1 juillet 1594, à Elmswell, Suffolk, Angleterre. Il a épousé Margery Everard environ 1619, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 4 avril 1665, à Branford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à North Branford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Robert Rose Sr wurde am 1. Juli 1594 in Elmswell, Suffolk, England geboren. Er hat um 1619 in England Margery Everard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 4. April 1665 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Branford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in North Branford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Robert Rose Sr è nato il il 1 luglio 1594 nel Elmswell, Suffolk, England. Lui sposato Margery Everard circa 1619, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 4 aprile 1665, nel Branford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolto a North Branford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Rose Srは1594年7月1日にイギリススフォルクエルムスウエルで生まれた。約1619年にイギリスでMargery Everardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1665年4月4日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンブランフォードで70歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンノース ブランフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.Branford, Connecticutc Colony, British C: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 브랜퍼드
ko.lifeSummary: Robert Rose Sr(은)는 1594년 7월 1일 영국 수폴크 엘스웰에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1619년 영국에서 Margery Everard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1665년 4월 4일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 브랜퍼드에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 노르스 브랜퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Robert Rose Sr nasceu em 1 de julho de 1594, em Elmswell, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Margery Everard aproximadamente 1619, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 4 de abril de 1665, em Branford, New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 70 anos, e foi sepultado em North Branford, New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Robert Rose Sr родился 1 июля 1594 г. в местности элмсвелл, суффолк, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery Everard около 1619 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Он умер 4 апреля 1665 г. в местности бранфорд, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности норт бранфорд, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Robert Rose Sr nació el 1 de julio de 1594, en Elmswell, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Se casó con Margery Everard aproximadamente 1619, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 4 de abril de 1665, en Branford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultado en North Branford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.
